reasoning: Iteration 1 regressed due to overly aggressive multi-category optimization (5 categories simultaneously) while correctness issues remained. Critical output formatting bug prevents validation. With 1 iteration left, must fix correctness first (semantic bug: output formatting), then apply ONE conservative high-impact optimization (SIMD vectorization of hot aggregation loop). Previous regression teaches: narrow focus is essential on final iteration.
focus_areas: Fix critical semantic bug: output o_orderdate as formatted YYYY-MM-DD string, not raw epoch integer
SIMD vectorization of aggregation hot loop (lines 371-386): vectorize l_shipdate filter and revenue calculation
strategy_notes: ITERATION 2 FINAL PUSH (1/1 remaining): (1) Apply critical semantic fix immediately - this unblocks validation and is low-risk. The output formatting bug (Date.value raw integer instead of formatted string) is the sole blocker to correct results. (2) Apply ONE high-impact performance optimization: SIMD vectorization of the aggregation inner loop. This is the highest-value target based on iter_1 analysis—processing 30M+ lineitem rows in scalar loop is main bottleneck. Do NOT attempt multiple optimizations across categories this time; iter_1 regression was caused by trying too much at once. (3) Expect execution time of ~1.4s → target ~700-900ms with SIMD (2x speedup on hot loop). If SIMD vectorization succeeds, Q3 reaches ~10-15x faster than current, approaching parity with GenDB baseline (386ms). If it regresses, rollback and accept 1.43s as final state (still 3.7x slower than baseline but correct).
notes: Conservative finisher: (1) Correctness FIRST - output format bug is the gate. (2) Narrow to ONE optimization category (cpu_bound: SIMD) to minimize regression risk. (3) Do not select recommendations from io_bound, aggregation, join, filter, or sort categories this iteration; iter_1 proved multi-category simultaneous optimization destabilizes the system with 1 iteration left. (4) Recommendation index 0 = critical semantic fix (output formatting), index 1 = SIMD vectorization (cpu_bound, high-impact, single-focused). (5) If this iteration passes validation and achieves >10% improvement, call it a win and stop. If it regresses or hits issues, rollback and stop.
